newsSinceJun6: No confirmed company-specific announcement since June 6. The +5.93% move on June 9 (TWD 71.50) appears flow-driven within the ongoing Taiwan memory rally: DRAM contract prices are up ~25% in 1Q26 and +10-12% in 2Q26, and management had previously guided that Q1 foundry price-hike benefits would start showing in June revenue. The structural items - Micron's $1.8B Tongluo P5 fab purchase (closed March 15, 2026), the long-term Micron alliance giving PSMC HBM supply-chain entry and a '3D AI Foundry' line, Q1 core-business return to profit, and the dividends-next-year pledge - all predate June 6.

sources.label: BigGo Finance - Powerchip operations passed the trough, Q1 core business profitable, price-hike benefits from June
BigGo Finance - Powerchip ends ten-quarter loss streak, teams with Micron for AI memory/HBM
StockTitan - Micron completes acquisition of PSMC's Tongluo P5 site (March 15, 2026)
cnyes - 6770 quote page (June 9 close +5.93%, no fresh company headlines)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a memory-tier alpha lens, a +5.9% day on top of +70% YTD (and ~+297% over 12 months) with no new fundamental evidence since June 6 is pure rerating chase - residual alpha shrinks. The HBM/Micron-alliance thesis is intact but is now well discovered and being paid for.
verdicts.bull: Micron alliance gives PSMC a real on-ramp into the HBM supply chain plus guaranteed orders, and Q1 price hikes flow into revenue from June, so the H2 earnings inflection is genuinely funded and underway.
verdicts.neutral: The turnaround (Q1 core profit, $1.36B of Micron cash received) is real but already public since March-April; today's move is sector beta, leaving the rank roughly fair after a small alpha haircut.
verdicts.bear: A mature-node foundry up ~4x in a year is priced for flawless HBM/3D-foundry execution; any memory-cycle rollover or slippage in the Micron collaboration unwinds the rerating fast.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June-onward monthly revenue showing the guided price-hike benefit, and concrete HBM/3D AI Foundry order milestones with Micron.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Memory-cycle peak or DRAM price-hike fatigue hitting a stock that has already rerated ~4x off the lows.

verdicts.rankRationale: Nothing since June 6 changes its CPO exposure, which is the lens of this page - the +5.9% pop is memory-rally flow, irrelevant to the 'low CPO direct exposure' thesis. Already ranked 110 with a low score, so hold with minor drift for the crowding/run-up.
verdicts.bull: If the Micron-backed 3D AI Foundry pivot eventually touches advanced packaging adjacent to optical I/O, PSMC could earn marginal CPO-ecosystem relevance it is not credited for today.
verdicts.neutral: It remains a foundry/memory name on a CPO list; the memory upcycle drives the stock but not this page's thesis, so rank near the bottom stays appropriate.
verdicts.bear: Zero incremental CPO content plus a crowded, fully rerated memory trade means this listing offers no asymmetry on this page and is held only for completeness.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any announced packaging or interposer work tied to optical/co-packaged interconnect via the Micron 3D AI Foundry roadmap.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Memory-cycle downturn dragging the stock with no CPO thesis to fall back on.
